known_facts: DataSync AI is a real-time data synchronization platform for enterprise retail
Median sync latency is 1.4 seconds versus Informatica's 3.9 hours
ARR is $8M, up from $3.5M a year ago (130% YoY growth)
Closed $18M Series A in February 2026, led by Bessemer Venture Partners
Previously raised $2.5M seed round in early 2024
Priya Nair spent 8 years at Oracle, including 4 years running the database replication product
Priya served as Chief Data Officer at a mid-sized retailer for 2 years
Team growing from 22 to 50 people in 2026
Expanding to UK and Germany in Q3 2026

hypothesized_angles.description: $18M Series A led by Bessemer — above TechCrunch threshold for B2B SaaS
hypothesized_angles.evidence: Founder confirmed $18M Series A in February 2026, led by Bessemer

hypothesized_angles.description: 1.4s sync latency vs 3.9-hour industry standard — 10,000x improvement — audited benchmark
hypothesized_angles.evidence: Head-to-head benchmark vs Informatica at a top-10 US grocery chain, audited by their data team

hypothesized_angles.description: $8M ARR milestone — strong for a 2-year-old B2B SaaS company
hypothesized_angles.evidence: Founder stated ARR just crossed $8M as of April 2026
Founder: 'we were at $3.5M a year ago, so we've basically grown 130% year-over-year'

hypothesized_angles.description: Only founder in the space with both Oracle replication engineering and retail CDO experience
hypothesized_angles.evidence: 8 years at Oracle (4 on database replication) + 2 years as retail CDO — 'maybe 50 people in the world who have done both'

hypothesized_angles.description: Top-10 US grocery chain as anchor customer paying $1.2M annual contract
hypothesized_angles.evidence: Founder confirmed customer is top-10 US grocery chain by revenue, $1.2M ACV — name not yet public

priority_questions.question: You mentioned the $18M Series A was led by Bessemer — can you tell me who else participated and what the total raised to date is?
priority_questions.follow_up_if_vague: Even just the total raised since founding would help — seed plus Series A?

priority_questions.question: The 1.4-second versus 3.9-hour benchmark is extraordinary. Can you share the methodology? Was this published or is it available for us to reference?
priority_questions.follow_up_if_vague: Would you be able to share the benchmark report, even under NDA, so we have something to cite?

priority_questions.question: You said there are maybe 50 people in the world who've done both Oracle replication and retail CDO roles. How did that unique combination directly shape the product architecture?
priority_questions.follow_up_if_vague: Can you give me a specific example of something you built into the product that someone without your background wouldn't have thought to build?

priority_questions.question: On the top-10 grocery chain — is there any chance they'd be willing to be referenced, even anonymously with their size and geography?
priority_questions.follow_up_if_vague: Even 'a top-10 US grocery retailer' as an attribution would significantly strengthen the story.

priority_questions.question: You mentioned zero data loss events across all 14 customers since launch. How long have you been in production?
priority_questions.follow_up_if_vague: Is there a total data volume or transaction count you can cite to contextualize that zero-loss claim?

interview_strategy: Lead with the funding announcement as the news hook, then pivot to the benchmark claim as the differentiator story. Push hard for the anchor customer name or at least permission to describe them. The founder's background story is a strong human angle — make sure to get the Oracle-to-retail-CDO narrative in her own words.
avoid_topics: Geographic expansion plan (already confirmed: UK + Germany Q3)
